components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Table S1. Composition of the anaerobic modified HS medium used for M. acetivorans culture ... Glucose 2.5 (g/L)

components.name: sodium sulfide nonahydrate (Na2S·9H2O)
components.evidence: Strain M. a 3fk was cultivated in 50 mL modified HS medium containing 10 mM Na2S in 100-mL serum bottles for 10 h. Then, 0.6 mM CdCl2 was added...

components.name: ammonium chloride (NH4Cl)
components.source: cited:same-paper Table S5 (standard HS medium) / Aldridge & Buan 2021 ref41 (19 mM NH4Cl)
components.evidence: Table S5 ... NH4Cl 1.000 (g/L); ref41: 'anaerobic high-salt (HS) medium (200 mM NaCl, 45 mM NaHCO3, 13 mM KCl, ... 19 mM NH4Cl, ...)'. NOTE: not enumerated in this paper's Table S1 but present in the same paper's Table S5 standard HS medium and the base HS medium.

components.name: L-cysteine·HCl
components.evidence: ref41: 'anaerobic high-salt (HS) medium (... 2.8 mM cysteine·HCl, 0.1 mM Na2S·9H2O, trace elements, vitamin solution)'. NOTE: part of the canonical HS base medium; NOT listed in this paper's Table S1/S5 and may be omitted in the glucose-modified version (which uses 10 mM Na2S as reductant).

components.name: resazurin
components.evidence: ref41: '... 2 µM 0.1% resazurin [w v−1] ...'. NOTE: part of the canonical HS base medium; not enumerated in this paper's Table S1/S5.

components.name: vitamin solution (unexpanded)
components.evidence: ref41: '... trace elements, vitamin solution'. NOTE: constituents not enumerated in any obtainable source for this recipe; canonical Methanosarcina HS vitamin solution is a Balch/Wolin-type mix but exact composition was NOT recoverable, so left unexpanded (no fabrication). Not listed in this paper's Table S1/S5.

media.base_medium: Anaerobic bicarbonate-buffered high-salt (HS) medium for Methanosarcina acetivorans C2A (Metcalf/Sowers lineage), modified with glucose + methanol; N2/CO2 headspace implied by bicarbonate buffering and serum-bottle cultivation.

sources_chased.what: SI Appendix Table S1 (the exact table this paper cites for the modified HS medium)
sources_chased.how: europePMC REST supplementaryFiles ZIP -> unzip -> pdftotext of pnas.2317058121.sapp.pdf
added: expanded trace/mineral solution: MnSO4, FeSO4, CoSO4, ZnSO4, CuSO4, AlK(SO4)2, H3BO3, Na2MoO4

sources_chased.what: SI Appendix Table S5 (same paper, standard HS + methanol 125 mM medium)
added: nitrilotriacetic acid 1.5 g/L chelator (standard medium only)

sources_chased.what: Base HS medium composition cited via same lab lineage: Aldridge, Carr, Weber, Buan 2021 (ref 41, PMC8104995)

missing_before: explicit trace-mineral salts (MnSO4·H2O, FeSO4·7H2O, CoSO4·7H2O, ZnSO4·7H2O, CuSO4·5H2O)
all concentrations (current_components were placeholder exchanges with no conc)
Na2S·9H2O (reductant, 10 mM, added for CdS biohybrid)
media.notes: Recipe recovered verbatim from the paper's own SI Appendix Table S1 (definitive), which gives exact g/L for every salt and expands the trace-element/mineral solution (classic Wolin/Balch-type mix). Glucose 2.5 g/L equals the 14 mM glucose stated in the main text. The paper's Table S1 does NOT list a nitrogen source, cysteine, resazurin, or a vitamin solution; NH4Cl (nitrogen) is taken from the same paper's Table S5 and confirmed at 19 mM in the base HS medium (ref 41, same Methanosarcina lab lineage). Cysteine·HCl, resazurin, and the vitamin solution belong to the canonical HS base medium (ref 41) but are absent from this paper's tables and may be intentionally omitted in the glucose-modified variant (which supplies 10 mM Na2S as reductant/CdS precursor); they are flagged accordingly. Vitamin solution left unexpanded per no-fabrication rule. Anaerobic, N2/CO2 headspace inferred from bicarbonate buffering and serum-bottle cultivation.
